title: Use StopAtEOF() to avoid early termination of following logs

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *jgallucci32* To complete the [pull request process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process), please assign **mrunalp** You can assign the PR to them by writing `/assign @mrunalp` in a comment when ready. The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Hi @jgallucci32. Thanks for your PR.

I'm waiting for a [containers](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) member to verify that this patch is reasonable to test. If it is, they should reply with `/ok-to-test` on its own line. Until that is done, I will not automatically test new commits in this PR, but the usual testing commands by org members will still work. Regular contributors should [join the org](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) to skip this step.

Once the patch is verified, the new status will be reflected by the `ok-to-test` label.

I understand the commands that are listed [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod).

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

/hold

2 checks failed. Seem to be the sames ones as before. No better, no worse.

@edsantiago I am able to reliably get this to fail with the following commands from the test
```
podman run --rm --name logs-f-rm -dt docker.io/library/alpine:latest sh -c 'echo podman; sleep 1; echo podman'
podman logs -f logs-f-rm
podman run --rm --name logs-f-rm -dt docker.io/library/alpine:latest true

```
NOTE: You have to run the 3 commands at the same time. Any delay in running the third command results in expected behavior.

This gives a good starting point to figure out where the race condition lives.

> @edsantiago I am able to reliably get this to fail with the following commands from the test
> 
> ```
> podman run --rm --name logs-f-rm -dt docker.io/library/alpine:latest sh -c 'echo podman; sleep 1; echo podman'
> podman logs -f logs-f-rm
> podman run --rm --name logs-f-rm -dt docker.io/library/alpine:latest true
> ```
> 
> NOTE: You have to run the 3 commands at the same time. Any delay in running the third command results in expected behavior.
> 
> This gives a good starting point to figure out where the race condition lives.

Ok it looks like PR https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/6614 fixes this condition. What I need to test is if `StopAtEOF()` in combination with event channels is the correct fix. It's possible it is waiting for DONE because event channels is faster and not completing the log to end of file.

There's no race condition, this is a broken, badly written test. `logs -f` should terminate when the container terminates, we all agree on that -- there is no reason whatsoever, though, for it to guarantee that the container has been removed and cleaned up. This e2e test needs to be rewritten.

@edsantiago Which specific test needs to be rewritten?

I'm thinking of closing this PR since it proved using `Stop()` and `StopAtEOF()` had to effect and this is being reworked in a separate PR.

/Close

This has been superseded by #6644 and #6614

@jgallucci32: Closed this PR.

<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/6632#issuecomment-645569171):

>/Close
>
>This has been superseded by #6644 and #6614

If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
